data.title: Pan American Silver Corp.: Volatility Presents An Opportunity For An Options Trade
data.description:
Introduction

My investments are confined to the precious metals sector only as I think that to diversify is also to dilute. This stance, of course, goes against the conventional wisdom of holding a spread of investments across a range of investment vehicles in the hope that if one sector suffers badly, the others will cushion the blow. I don’t disagree with this philosophy as it suits most investors; it just so happens not to suit me as it doesn’t fit with my investment criteria. You are a unique person with your unique investment criteria, and as such, you must decide what suits you and fits in with your financial goals, your time horizon, your aversion to risk, etc.

I acquired physical gold and physical silver many moons ago, and in the recent past, my focus has been on the mining sector. My current position is that I'm fully invested in the mining companies that, I think, will do well, and I'm now looking to juice the portfolio via several options trades.

So far this year, the mining companies have done really well, but it's not without considerable volatility, and that volatility can be an options trader's dream or, if you get it wrong, a trader's nightmare, so you have been warned.

I've looked at a number of companies that, I think, are suitable candidates for this trade, and I've recently traded Pan American Silver Corp. (NYSE:PAAS), which was successful as it returned a profit of 288%. I didn’t publish an article dedicated to this trade due to time constraints, but I did write about it in the comments section of the last article I published on Pan American Silver, which was entitled:

“Pan American Silver Q2 2024 Results Suggest That The Best Is Still To Come” which may be of interest to you.

I am currently holding several call options on Wheaton Precious Metals Corp. (WPM), which I will also comment on as this trade unfolds.

So, for my next trade, I have selected Pan American Silver Corp to be the vehicle as, on a YTD basis, it has oscillated considerably, as shown on the chart below.

Pan American Silver Corp.: Brief Description

Pan American is one of the largest silver producers in the world and also derives income from gold production as well as the production of zinc, lead, and copper.

It has a market capitalization of $10.05 billion with an average trading volume of 4.6 million shares per day. Its current EPS stands at $0.86, and its P/E ratio is 33.11.

Pan American has been a solid performer so far this year, as the Q1 Consolidated Q1 2025 Results indicate. The standouts for me are that Silver production reached 5.0 million ounces, Gold production came in at 182.2 thousand ounces, and the Revenue achieved was $773.2 million. Also of interest to me is the cost of production, where they recorded the All-in Sustaining Costs (“AISC”) for silver at $13.94 per ounce. Given that silver is trading at $36.00 per ounce, the stage is set for a bright second half of 2025.

According to the Silver Institute, this metal is in a state of what we call a ‘Structural Deficit’, which I covered in an article entitled “Silver's Structural Deficit Suggests Higher Prices Ahead”. As for gold, well, it has been leading the way through this rally in the precious metals sector, making several all-time highs along the way. When we consider that the world is knee-deep in debt and the money printing machine continues to generate more and more Fiat currency, then the move by some investors to hard assets is understandable, and I expect that it will continue for some time to come.

If we take a quick look at Seeking Alpha's database, we can see that Pan American has an EPS (forward) of $1.61 and a P/E (forward) of 17.21, which suggests a significant improvement from its current position and if correct, bodes very well for the future of Pan American.

From Seeking Alpha’s Quant section, we can glean that it has a rating of a ‘strong buy’, the Wall Street analysts have a rating of ‘buy’, and Seeking Alpha analysts have a rating of ‘hold’, so they are all quite positive in their view of Pan American Silver Corp as a good investment.

Pan American is listed on the NYSE and the TSX.

This article is not intended to cover this company in detail; the intention is to give the reader an appreciation of why I have chosen this company as a vehicle for an Options Trade. There are a number of close contenders for this sort of candidacy, and no doubt, as the year progresses, I will be looking to utilize them for an Options Trading purpose.

Pan American Silver Corp.: YTD Progress Chart

2025 has been volatile so far as the stock price can gain or lose almost 50% of its value in a short time.

The technical indicators were in the overbought zone in early June but have now unwound that position.

This is just one of my many quirks, but that gap, which was formed at the beginning of June, does make me a tad nervous, as sooner or later, the price tends to return and backfill it before moving on. This does not happen in every case, but it is something to be aware of.

The triple witching hour of month end, quarter end, and half year-end has now passed, so I am expecting the precious metals sector to resume its trek to higher ground and hope to close this trade before a correction comes along and dumps it into the red.

A Quick Look at A One-Year Chart of Gold Prices

Gold's rally started in March 2024 and has recently traded at $3,500/Oz, followed by a breather, which may now be over.

The MACD was in the overbought zone, suggesting a near-term correction, which has now arrived.

The RSI is out of the overbought zone, suggesting that this correction is now over.

A Quick Look at A One-Year Chart of Silver Prices

Silver has formed a double top at just above $34.00/Oz and has now managed to edge above this level to trade at $36.02, which it needs to hold for now.

This chart demonstrates the wild oscillations in the silver price. The technical indicators were in the overbought zone but have now eased back a tad, suggesting that this price level for silver could be held in the short term.

Conclusion

I've chosen PAAS Jan 2026 35.000 call options, which I acquired for an average price of $1.63 per contract.

I did consider buying a contract that was “On The Money”, which lessened the risk considerably. However, the purchase price is around $3.50, and in my experience, the percentage gain relative to the stock price is usually lower.

The stock price of PAAS at the time of writing is around $29.00, and we could argue that for PAAS to get a price of $35.00 is a bit of a stretch, which is true. However, that is not my intention, as I will consider closing this trade on any spike in the stock price that returns a reasonable profit in a short time period. If, for instance, we get a $2.00 increase in the stock price in the coming week or so, this particular Call Option could increase in value by 30%, and I would find it hard to resist not banking it. However, as explained earlier, I did let the previous trade run, and it returned a handsome profit. Your exit price depends on you and your aversion to risk etc., and we are all different in that respect. On the downside, a sudden fall in the price of the underlying commodities of gold and silver or some negative news about Pan American which causes the stock price to drop significantly can reduce a Call Option to zero, so do not risk too much of your investment capital, as you need to be able to sleep at night, and live to fight another day. Also important to note is that once you have purchased an Options Contract the price you paid for it is the maximum that you can lose, so the downside is limited.

I selected the January series as it is six months away, allowing time for the rally in precious metals to resume, which is beneficial to the already profitable stocks in this sector. These particular call options are known as ‘Out Of The Money’ as they have no intrinsic value. There are 100 shares per contract, so each contract costs $1.63 X 100 = $163 each. Also, note that the spread between the Bid price and Offer price can be rather wide at times.

You can now have a chuckle as I try to wangle a profit out of this trade in the weeks and months ahead.

Go gently.

data.title: Centene: Even Slashing Guidance May Not Be Enough
data.description:
Centene: 40% Drop! Well Deserved

You didn't manage to sell the stock of Centene Corporation (NYSE:CNC) before it plummeted 40% this week? So, what now? Should you rush in to catch the falling knife? Should you wait for the dust to settle first? Or should you accept your decision to hang on to it through the hammering?

To be clear, I think the market has already inflicted substantive damage on CNC as I pen this write-up. Why?

  • CNC has fallen by another 1.4% as we head to the Fourth of July weekend, while the S&P 500 (SPX) (SP500) reached another new high. Going the opposite way (against the market) has never felt so distinct, painful, and surreal for CNC holders.
  • The wipeout this week has taken CNC back to the days of early 2017. A reminder that the market has taken Centene's decision to withdraw its 2025 guidance extremely seriously.
  • Valuation for CNC has already collapsed to 5x on a forward earnings basis, significantly below the 7.3x that marks the -2 standard deviation zone under its 10Y average. Is that enough? I will address that subsequently.

Nevertheless, I hope to have impressed upon you that the market isn't foolish. The market is trying to price in even more uncertainties as execution risks on Centene's primarily Medicaid-driven business model are undergoing incredible challenges. Health insurance companies have endured a terrible 2025, with UnitedHealth (UNH) already dropping to multi-year lows.

And the fire has now spread to the leading Medicaid players, compounded by the budget cuts informed by Congress passing Trump's "One, Big Beautiful Bill," sending it to the Oval Office for him to sign into law. The moment of reckoning seems to have crystallized for health insurance companies under fire this year, taking hits after hits, and why should we expect Centene to be able to avoid them?

Centene: Brace For 50% Cut To 2025 Adjusted EPS

Next, Wall Street isn't foolish. Calls have been made to suggest that Centene's adjusted EPS could be slashed by 50% from its initial prognosis of $7.25 for FY2025 (which is, of course, no longer viable given the guidance withdrawal). Management has alluded to a reduction of $2.75 from its adjusted EPS, pointing to a downward revision to less than $5. Hence, the base EPS reset obviously afflicted the company's projections because it sets Centene back by nearly 40% from its initial outlook. And the stock fell by 40%? It was quite appropriate, right?

As a reminder, we should take into account that Centene cautioned that the downward revisions may not be over. First, I must accord due credit to management for helping to quantify the assessed impact on its adjusted earnings. Because that really helps the market to navigate with a little more clarity, as compared to asking investors to "guesstimate" how wide or deep the extent of the cuts could amount to. Now, we can focus on:

  • Additional impacts from the other seven marketplace states that Centene is involved with, but has yet to reflect the updated data into its calculations. However, it has already informed that downgrades should be expected, given the data on the current 22 marketplace states that spurred its decision to pull guidance.
  • Investors can now think about the anticipated pricing changes from next year. Analysts will also try and parse plausible clarity from management at CNC's upcoming Q2 earnings call on the guideposts for their modeling, and also afford insights into whether it could set the low watermark for 2025. In other words, I think investors may start asking questions about whether a recovery to the $5 mark for its adjusted EPS could be viable.

Still, several risks could still unhinge Centene's thesis, even as it has attempted to diversify beyond the Medicaid business. Notably, Medicaid comprises more than 65% of its revenue in Q1 over the past 12 months. Next, Medicaid's gross profit was about 33% of its total base in Q1 over the past 12 months. But, with the latest updates on slower growth and encumbered with higher cost headwinds on its marketplace businesses, the double whammy may impinge on any near-term recovery prospects for a while.

Therefore, for investors to think about whether investors could reasonably project $5 as the adjusted EPS hurdle for management to clear becomes increasingly pivotal. Analyst estimates have not reflected the full damage yet, so we need to be careful here and try to ascertain whether an inflection point from 2026 can be attained.

Buy Or Sell CNC Stock?

Note that 2026 adjusted EPS forecasts still point to $7.11 (according to S&P Cap IQ data). On a next 12 months basis, the adjusted EPS is still at the $6.6 level. I suspect these are no longer viable. Hence, if we assess $5 as a possible 2026 adjusted EPS for us to model, then CNC's 2026 P/E multiple should be reflected as 6.7x.

And if we compared it to the 7.3x level that marked its highly pessimistic zone, that's still a relative discount of about 8%. Does it sound reasonable enough? Well, I would say the market has arguably done its job, and that hammering is conceivably justified to reflect the downgrade. However, it's also clear that CNC has dropped to levels lower than 6x before, although not too often over the past ten years.

So, I think we must be circumspect when we contemplate that the regulatory and political environment for Centene's primary ACA and Medicaid business is facing noticeable structural constraints that could hinder a near-term recovery.

UNH stock has also struggled for traction amidst its own challenges earlier this year. All this while, the S&P 500 has been gaining incredible momentum to take out new highs, while health insurance investors suffer one fallout after another.

For now, I think it might be better to listen and attune to the market. While CNC is arguably cheap, it sure looks like it's cheap for (very good) reasons, with the hammering well validated and apt.

Rating: Hold.
